core.label: Ruby Fundamentals
core.description: Syntax, types, and the object-oriented basics.

topics.label: Installation & IRB
topics.description: Install Ruby with rbenv/asdf and use IRB for interactive exploration. IRB is Ruby's REPL.

topics.label: Variables & Data Types
topics.description: Integers, floats, strings, symbols, booleans, nil. Everything in Ruby is an object.

topics.label: Strings & Symbols
topics.description: String interpolation, methods, and immutable symbols. Symbols are used as hash keys and identifiers.

topics.label: Arrays
topics.description: Dynamic arrays with rich methods — map, select, reject, each, reduce, flatten, compact.

topics.label: Hashes
topics.description: Key-value pairs with symbol keys and string keys. Hashes are Ruby's dictionary type.

topics.label: Control Flow
topics.description: if/elsif/else, unless, case/when, while, until, for, and ternary. unless and until are Ruby-specific.

topics.label: Methods
topics.description: Define methods with def, default parameters, and implicit return. The last expression is the return value.

topics.label: Blocks, Procs & Lambdas
topics.description: Pass code blocks with {} or do/end, create Proc and lambda objects. Blocks are central to Ruby idioms.

topics.label: Iterators & Enumerable
topics.description: each, map, select, reject, reduce, any?, all?, find. Enumerable is Ruby's most powerful mixin.

topics.label: Ranges
topics.description: Create ranges with .. and ... for iteration, slicing, and conditions.

core.label: Object-Oriented Ruby
core.description: Classes, modules, mixins, and Ruby's object model.

topics.label: Classes & Objects
topics.description: Define classes with initialize, instance variables (@), and methods. Everything is an object in Ruby.

topics.label: Attr Accessors
topics.description: Generate getters/setters with attr_reader, attr_writer, attr_accessor. Reduces boilerplate.

topics.label: Inheritance
topics.description: Single inheritance with <, super, and method overriding. Ruby has single class inheritance.

topics.label: Modules & Mixins
topics.description: Share behavior across classes with include and extend. Modules solve the single-inheritance limitation.

topics.label: Access Control
topics.description: public, private, protected methods. Private means private to the instance in Ruby.

topics.label: Duck Typing
topics.description: Ruby cares about what an object can do, not what it is. respond_to? checks capabilities.

topics.label: Open Classes & Monkey Patching
topics.description: Reopen and modify existing classes at runtime. Powerful but use responsibly.

topics.label: Comparable & Enumerable
topics.description: Include Comparable for sorting and Enumerable for collection methods on your classes.

topics.label: Struct & Data
topics.description: Quick value objects with Struct and immutable Data (Ruby 3.2+). Reduce class boilerplate.

core.label: Error Handling & I/O
core.description: Exceptions, file operations, and working with data.

topics.label: Exceptions
topics.description: begin/rescue/ensure/raise for error handling. Ruby exceptions are objects inheriting from Exception.

topics.label: Custom Exceptions
topics.description: Create domain-specific error classes inheriting from StandardError.

topics.label: File I/O
topics.description: Read and write files with File.read, File.write, and block form for auto-closing.

topics.label: JSON & YAML
topics.description: Parse and generate JSON and YAML. JSON is standard for APIs, YAML for configuration.

topics.label: Regular Expressions
topics.description: Pattern matching with /regex/ and =~ operator. Ruby has first-class regex support.

topics.label: String Processing
topics.description: gsub, scan, split, match, encode. Advanced string manipulation methods.

topics.label: Metaprogramming Basics
topics.description: define_method, method_missing, send, respond_to_missing?. Ruby's dynamic nature enables powerful metaprogramming.

topics.label: Pattern Matching
topics.description: Structural pattern matching with case/in (Ruby 3+). Match arrays, hashes, and custom objects.

core.label: Gems, Bundler & Testing
core.description: Package management, testing, and code quality.

topics.label: RubyGems & Bundler
topics.description: Manage dependencies with Gemfile and Bundler. RubyGems is Ruby's package system.

topics.label: RSpec
topics.description: Behavior-driven testing with describe, it, expect. RSpec is the most popular Ruby testing framework.

topics.label: Minitest
topics.description: Ruby's built-in testing framework. Simpler than RSpec with both assertion and spec styles.

topics.label: Mocking & Stubbing
topics.description: Isolate tests with RSpec mocks or Mocha. Mock external dependencies for fast, reliable tests.

topics.label: RuboCop
topics.description: Lint and auto-format Ruby code. RuboCop enforces the Ruby Style Guide.

topics.label: Debugging (debug gem)
topics.description: Step-through debugging with Ruby's built-in debug gem. Set breakpoints with binding.break.

topics.label: Rake
topics.description: Ruby's build tool for task automation. Define tasks in Rakefiles.

topics.label: Concurrency (Ractor & Fiber)
topics.description: Ractors for parallel execution and Fibers for cooperative concurrency. Ruby 3 added true parallelism.
